skill.name: Azure.AI.VoiceLive (.NET)
skill.description: Build real-time voice AI applications with Azure AI VoiceLive SDK for .NET. Enables bidirectional WebSocket voice communication for assistants and conversational AI.
skill.summary: Real-time voice AI SDK documentation for building bidirectional voice assistants with Azure AI.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines and detected no security issues. This is a documentation-only skill (SKILL.md) providing guidance for using the Azure AI VoiceLive SDK. No executable code, network calls, or file system access patterns are present in the skill itself. The skill recommends secure authentication practices using DefaultAzureCredential.

content.user_title: Build Real-Time Voice AI Apps with Azure VoiceLive
content.value_statement: Developers struggle to implement real-time voice AI features with proper authentication and event handling. This skill provides complete .NET SDK documentation for Azure VoiceLive, including secure authentication, WebSocket session management, and bidirectional audio streaming patterns.
actual_capabilities: Guide implementation of Azure AI VoiceLive SDK for real-time voice applications
Configure secure authentication using DefaultAzureCredential or API keys
Set up WebSocket session configuration for bidirectional voice communication
Implement event handling for audio deltas, text responses, and function calls
Define and handle function calling within voice sessions
Configure voice options, turn detection, and audio formats
limitations: Does not execute code directly - provides documentation and guidance only
Requires valid Azure AI resource with VoiceLive enabled
Does not handle audio capture or playback - requires external libraries like NAudio
Does not manage Azure resource provisioning or billing

use_cases.title: Voice Assistant Development
use_cases.description: Build conversational voice assistants that process speech in real-time and respond with synthesized audio and text.

use_cases.title: Real-Time Speech-to-Speech Translation
use_cases.description: Create applications that capture voice input, process it through AI models, and output translated speech with minimal latency.

use_cases.title: Voice-Enabled Chatbots
use_cases.description: Integrate natural voice interaction into existing chatbot systems using Azure AI VoiceLive for hands-free user experiences.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Voice Session Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a basic Azure VoiceLive session in .NET. I need to authenticate with DefaultAzureCredential, configure the session with text and audio modalities, and handle incoming audio events.

prompt_templates.title: Function Calling Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to define and handle function calls in Azure VoiceLive. I want to add a weather lookup function that the voice assistant can call during conversations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding tool usage to voice sessions

prompt_templates.title: Custom Voice and Turn Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure Azure VoiceLive with a custom neural voice and semantic voice activity detection. Set appropriate silence duration and threshold values for natural conversation flow.

prompt_templates.title: Full Voice Assistant Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete real-time voice assistant example using Azure VoiceLive SDK. Include authentication, session management, event handling loop, error handling, and function calling for external APIs.

output_examples.input: I want to build a voice assistant that can check the weather
output: VoiceLiveClient connects to Azure AI endpoint using DefaultAzureCredential
Session configured with voice modality and weather function definition
User speaks query, audio sent via WebSocket to Azure
Function call triggered, weather API response sent back to session
Assistant responds with spoken weather information

output_examples.input: How do I handle errors in VoiceLive sessions
output: Cancellation failed errors can be safely ignored
Network errors should trigger session reconnection logic

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication instead of hardcoded API keys
Configure both Text and Audio modalities for complete voice assistant functionality
Always wrap VoiceLiveSession in using statement for proper resource disposal
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in source code - use environment variables or managed identity
Do not omit error handling for SessionUpdateError events
Do not skip disposing VoiceLiveSession - always use using statement

faq.question: What Azure resources do I need to use VoiceLive SDK
faq.answer: You need an Azure AI Services resource with VoiceLive enabled. Assign the Cognitive Services User role for managed identity authentication, or obtain an API key from the Azure Portal.

faq.question: Does VoiceLive support custom voices
faq.answer: Yes, VoiceLive supports Azure Standard voices, Azure HD voices, and Azure Custom voices. Use AzureStandardVoice for built-in voices or AzureCustomVoice with an endpoint ID for custom neural voices.

faq.question: What audio format does VoiceLive require
faq.answer: VoiceLive uses PCM 16-bit audio at 24kHz sample rate in mono. Set InputAudioFormat and OutputAudioFormat to Pcm16 in your session configuration.

faq.question: Can VoiceLive handle multiple languages
faq.answer: Yes, VoiceLive supports multiple languages through voice selection. Specify language-region voice identifiers like en-US-AvaNeural or configure custom voices for other languages.

faq.question: How does function calling work in VoiceLive
faq.answer: Define VoiceLiveFunctionDefinition with JSON schema parameters, add to session options Tools collection, then handle SessionUpdateResponseFunctionCallArgumentsDone events to process calls and send FunctionCallOutputItem responses.

faq.question: What is the difference between VoiceLive and Cognitive Services Speech SDK
faq.answer: VoiceLive provides real-time bidirectional voice AI with GPT-4o models for conversational assistants. Cognitive Services Speech SDK handles speech-to-text and text-to-speech separately without integrated AI reasoning.
